specific_location: On the ice at Valmont Reservoir.
description: First, my trip report, including a brief written description of the species, can be found here: http://tiny.cc/OXM2U  Note that there were 2 Mew Gulls out there. This report does NOT concern the adult, which I believe has been previously documented--with photographs--by Bill Schmoker. This report concerns only the first-cycle individual, which has not been previously reported to my knowledge.  The bird was small and notably petite in contrast with Ring-billed Gulls sitting and standing immediately next to it. It was dusky all over, with a notably dainty bill. The flight feathers of the wing, as seen on the sitting bird, were darker than the rest of the upperparts; however, the overall appearance of the bird was one of low-contrast, extensive duskiness.Behaviors: I saw it sitting and standing.Call: None heard.Plumage: first-cycle
